race.name: Granfondo Dobbiaco-Cortina
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers along the old railway through the heart of the Dolomites — Italian skiing at its most scenic and civilized.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations with Italian refreshments including vin brule

climate.description: The Dolomites deliver cold, stable January-February conditions at 1,200 meters altitude. Temperatures range from -12°C to 0°C. The sheltered valley route along the old railway provides protection from alpine winds. Snow reliability at this altitude is excellent, with the Dolomites receiving consistent winter precipitation.
challenges: Cold starts in the shaded Dobbiaco valley
Altitude at 1,200m requires acclimatization for lowland skiers
Occasional Foehn winds can raise temperatures rapidly
Sun exposure on south-facing sections creates variable snow texture

course.primary: Point-to-point along the converted old railway line from Dobbiaco to Cortina d'Ampezzo, passing through tunnels and crossing the dramatic Dolomite landscape. The gentle downhill gradient of the old railway makes this a fast, relatively flat course surrounded by some of the most spectacular mountain scenery in the Alps.
course.surface: Impeccably groomed trails on the old railway corridor
course.grooming: World-class Italian standard — the old railway bed provides a perfect grooming surface
features: Follows the historic Dobbiaco-Cortina railway line through the Dolomites
UNESCO World Heritage Dolomite scenery throughout
Gentle net downhill makes for fast times
Finishes in Cortina d'Ampezzo — site of the 2026 Winter Olympics
Both classic and skate tracks available

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Perfect scores in grooming and scenery — the Dolomite railway route is both impeccably maintained and breathtakingly beautiful. Strong international draw from Cortina's fame and the 2026 Olympics connection. Minimal elevation gain keeps the physical challenge modest.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 eligible for 1-tier promotion, already at T2

history.summary: Founded in 1977, the Granfondo Dobbiaco-Cortina follows the route of the decommissioned railway line that once connected the South Tyrolean town of Dobbiaco (Toblach) with the glamorous resort of Cortina d'Ampezzo. The converted railway corridor provides a uniquely scenic and well-graded course through UNESCO World Heritage Dolomite landscape. With Cortina hosting events at the 2026 Winter Olympics, the race's profile has never been higher.
notable_facts: Follows the decommissioned Dobbiaco-Cortina railway line
Course passes through original railway tunnels
Dolomites are a UNESCO World Heritage Site
Cortina d'Ampezzo hosts 2026 Winter Olympic events
The route crosses from German-speaking South Tyrol into Italian-speaking Veneto
One of the most scenic marathon ski courses in the world

youtube_data.search_query: Granfondo Dobbiaco-Cortina cross country ski race Dobbiaco to Cortina d'Ampezzo

The Ski Classics Challengers events, Granfondo Dobbiaco/Toblach - Cortina Classic and Skating, took place last weekend, featuring a 26km-long-distance ski race in Toblach, Italy. Watch the highlights here!

videos.curation_reason: Official race coverage with athlete interviews and detailed course footage from the historic 45th edition.

videos.title: Cross Country Skiing in Dobbiaco
videos.description: Cross Country Skiing in Dobbiaco offers the right track for everyone. It also guarantees snow and perfectly prepared tracks. As venue for international World Cup races, Dobbiaco benefits from 120 km of perfectly prepared cross country skiing trails for all abilities. Whether you train for ski marathons, want to push yourself to your limits on the FIS-standardised tracks or prefer to glide late at night on the lit night track (from Christmas daily from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m.): Cross Country Skiing in Dobbiaco is uniquely beautiful. The cross country ski tracks of Dobbiaco are also connected with the rest of the holiday region 3 Zinnen in the Dolomites, allowing you to ski on a total of 200 km of tracks. The cross country track pass 3 Zinnen Dolomites also gives you access to the network in Cortina.

quotes.text: When you saw the mountain you can enjoy the landscape sometimes when you felt bad you can control the landscape and you tell yourself that you are lucky to be there and it's nice.
It was tough race from the beginning and then in the downhill we had several attacks and the group stayed together with four guys and luckily in the sprint in the end I was the fastest.
